# Transcrição: 04-Why Do People Hire Coaches

- **Fonte:** 04-Why Do People Hire Coaches
- **Duração:** 08:36
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Have you ever actually purchased a tiger? Like bought a tiger? Other people have. Like they bought zoos. Did you know that? Okay, so in this lesson we're not going to be buying exotic animals or zoos, but I really do want to talk to you about a very important question before we dive into a whole bunch of other conversations about premium coaching, and that's why would somebody want to hire a coach at all? You know, when I first started, I had this vision that I would get paid to be an amazing coach, and at first I wanted to focus on helping people with their productivity. But the thing is, is I had never hired someone to help me with productivity. I had listened to a lot of podcasts. I had taken a bunch of courses. I had read all the books. I was a straight up nerd about productivity, and I had this idea that I wanted to be paid as a coach for productivity, but I wasn't so sold on whether anybody had ever been hired as a productivity coach. So I want to address that right now with you, because you may or may not have ever hired a coach, and if you have, you may or may not have hired a coach for what it is that you do. And I bring up the Tigers and the Zews and stuff, because it turns out there's a lot of things that you probably have never bought that other people have. So let's just bust the doors open right now on the myth that in order for you to be successful in selling this thing, you have had to have bought it. I mean, there's so many things I've never bought before that other people have bought plenty of. And isn't that true for you too? So why do people hire coaches? Well, one reason is they want another path. They've been wanting to solve a challenge, or they've had this ambition in their heart for a really long time, and maybe they've gathered a lot of information, but they want transformation. They want to go to that next level, but they haven't been able to yet, or maybe they've worked with someone else, and they've gone through a different process, and they're not there yet. Maybe they were like me, and they just lacked some social support in that particular area of growth. Have you ever felt like you really wanted to go to that next level in a particular area,

## [03:04 - 06:08]

but you kind of had people around you look at you like you were kind of weird for it? That's a perfect place for hiring a coach. People tend to hire a coach when they feel like they're the only one, the only one in their social circle, or their town, or their community who has the dream on their heart, or their desire, or their circumstances to go to that next level in that next place. I like to think of it as the people who feel like they're the only one, and they're stuck with kind of that broken wing feeling, kind of they're stuck, broken wing in the nest, and they can't move on. Then there's the only one who's like that eagle soaring alone. Coaches, you want to be working with those eagles. You're going to be very, very tempting to fall in love with rescuing the only broken wing, but if you put a lot of broken wing messaging out there, you're going to be attracting a lot of broken wing clients, and that's not going to be a premium practice. People who have ambition to change are going to be the people who are most likely to actually hire a coach, and they're going to want to change their circumstances. People who have a uniqueness to their journey, it feels unique, they want a coach because they want someone who can help them see the distinction in their process. They want someone who maybe is going to be able to honor the privacy in their process. A lot of times, the clients that have hired me over the years, they don't want it disclosed on social media that they hired me because maybe they are particularly wealthy individuals, or maybe they are working through something that when they're on the other side of it, they're going to be super happy to give a testimonial, but at the beginning of the process, they want that privacy. I know for me, when I was starting my entrepreneurial journey, I wanted a private coach because I didn't really want my workplace to know what I was working on yet. There's a lot of opportunity for private coaching simply because a lot of people want that confidential space to be able to work with someone. I would say one of the chief things that makes coaching such a powerful tool versus, say, a group program or a course environment or even a mastermind is they want a witness, like a for sure witness, someone to see what they're going through who has the experience,

## [06:08 - 08:36]

the expertise, and the structure. This is different than, say, a friend or even a therapist or someone who is going to maybe see what they're doing, but not offer challenge, not offer that perspective of the progress that a coach would be able to offer. They want to be able to trust someone with the secrets of their success. That might include their stress, their struggles, and even their breakthroughs. There's so many breakthroughs on the way to success. When you're doing something that's really big, you're going to have a lot of failure on the way. The more you feel like a failure on the way, the harder it is to motivate yourself to keep going. That's one of the biggest roles a coach can play is help you with the reframe of failure and help you with praiseworthy failure versus blameworthy failure. They want someone who's going to be there to remind them of their progress, help them gain perspective, and help them set the pace. This is one of the biggest things that a premium coach can do is push the client. Instead of sitting there and waiting for the client to bring them problems or bring them, hey, what do you want me to keep you accountable for? Hey, what are we working on this week? A premium coach is going to help set a bar and let the client know, hey, you have it in you to go to that next level, to set a higher standard. The coach is going to be able to recommend content or books or exercises or things to actually help the client build practices that will improve their pace and will improve the quality of their work. Those are some of the many reasons why people hire coaches. I would love to know what are some of the other reasons that you could think of that someone might hire a coach or maybe some reasons that you've hired a coach in the past. In the next lesson, we're going to talk about why would they hire you.
